C01.title: Vendor-neutral agent skill and TASK-PLAN PRO module

C02.title: Accepted concept to validated operation graph

blocks.title: Build and validate the operation graph
blocks.main_output: Source-bound canonical operation graph and passing readiness audit

blocks.title: Generate and review deterministic projections
blocks.main_output: Self-contained local review workspace and hash-bound build manifest

nodes.title: Build and validate the operation graph

operational_fields.what: A compatible coding agent decomposes an accepted concept into canonical graph data, and the CLI validates structure, meaning, traceability, routes, and source identity.
operational_fields.where: The agent skill operates in the user's coding environment; deterministic validation runs locally through the packaged Python engine.
operational_fields.when: This block starts after the product owner identifies and accepts the current concept version.
operational_fields.why: It prevents attractive diagrams or oversized implementation tasks from hiding missing product meaning and broken transitions.
operational_fields.input: An accepted product concept, a target graph path, repository evidence, and explicit output boundaries.
operational_fields.input_requirements: The concept is readable, version-identifiable, owner-accepted, and free of unresolved meaning that only the owner can decide.
operational_fields.expected_output: A canonical operation graph bound to the concept hash plus an audit with a deterministic readiness verdict.
operational_fields.success_criteria: The validator exits zero, reports ready true, covers every required concept section, and finds no missing gate or failure route.
operational_fields.failure_criteria: Validation finds a semantic gap, traceability gap, source mismatch, invalid route, unsupported implementation claim, or malformed graph.
operational_fields.failure_action: Return only the affected graph fragment to the responsible agent or return an owner-owned semantic decision to the product owner.
operational_fields.executor: Compatible coding agent using the TASKPLAN PRO Operation Map skill and local CLI.
operational_fields.acceptor: Product owner for meaning and deterministic validator for structural readiness.
operational_fields.resources: Published skill directory, Python 3.10 or newer, repository evidence, and operation-map contracts.
operational_fields.evidence: Canonical graph hash, concept hash, readiness audit, validator exit code, and repository paths supporting implementation claims.
operational_fields.next_transition: A passing readiness gate admits the exact graph bytes to deterministic projection generation.

operational_fields.what: Validate the exact canonical graph against the accepted concept and closed readiness rules.
operational_fields.where: Inside the local operation-map validator before any review projection is generated.
operational_fields.when: After graph authoring or any correction changes canonical graph bytes.
operational_fields.why: Derived views must never make an incomplete or stale graph appear ready for downstream work.
operational_fields.input: Canonical graph JSON, accepted concept bytes, declared section registry, and repository evidence references.
operational_fields.input_requirements: Graph and concept hashes resolve, required fields are populated, and all referenced IDs and evidence paths are attributable.
operational_fields.expected_output: A source-bound audit that reports ready true or lists exact blocking findings.
operational_fields.success_criteria: The audit contains zero errors and the validation process exits with code zero.
operational_fields.failure_criteria: Any structural, semantic, traceability, routing, evidence, or source-identity error remains.
operational_fields.failure_action: Route the exact finding to B01-F01 and correct only the narrowest responsible source or request an owner decision.
operational_fields.reviewer: Independent graph reviewer verifies that passing structure does not overstate product truth.
operational_fields.acceptor: Readiness policy encoded by the packaged validator.
operational_fields.resources: operation_map.py, operation-map schema, accepted concept, and canonical graph.
operational_fields.evidence: Validator exit code and OPERATION-MAP-AUDIT.json bound to graph and concept hashes.
operational_fields.next_transition: Success unblocks B02; failure opens B01-F01.

operational_fields.what: Preserve the failed verdict and identify the narrowest graph, evidence, or owner-decision boundary responsible for it.
operational_fields.where: The audit report and agent handoff for the current graph revision.
operational_fields.when: Whenever the readiness gate returns one or more blocking errors.
operational_fields.why: A visible correction route prevents silent retries, invented product meaning, and unrelated scope expansion.
operational_fields.input: Failed readiness audit with stable finding codes and affected paths.
operational_fields.input_requirements: Findings retain graph identity, source hashes, and exact paths; no error is replaced by a generic retry request.
operational_fields.expected_output: A bounded correction to the responsible graph fragment or an explicit unresolved owner decision.
operational_fields.success_criteria: The responsible boundary is identified and the next validation run uses corrected, source-consistent inputs.
operational_fields.failure_criteria: The agent invents missing semantics, changes unrelated scope, or retries without addressing the recorded finding.
operational_fields.failure_action: Stop automated correction and return to B01 with the unresolved finding and required authority named.
operational_fields.executor: Responsible coding agent or product owner according to the finding type.
operational_fields.acceptor: Product owner for semantic corrections and validator for mechanical corrections.
operational_fields.resources: Failed audit, canonical sources, repository evidence, and bounded correction policy.
operational_fields.evidence: Before-and-after source hashes, resolved finding codes, and the subsequent validator result.
operational_fields.next_transition: Return to B01 and rerun readiness after the bounded correction is complete.

operational_fields.what: Generate Markdown, presentation, locale, build-manifest, and self-contained HTML projections from the admitted canonical graph.
operational_fields.where: The CLI builds files locally; Codex, Claude Code, or another compatible agent may orchestrate the skill without changing projection semantics.
operational_fields.when: This block starts only after B01 readiness passes for the exact graph and concept hashes.
operational_fields.why: Humans and external interfaces need an inspectable view without creating a second source of product truth.
operational_fields.input: Readiness-admitted graph, accepted concept, optional presentation data, locale catalog, and review state.
operational_fields.input_requirements: Every derived input matches the graph hash and no comment or project-specific UI value is silently promoted into canonical meaning.
operational_fields.expected_output: Deterministic projection files, a local multi-reviewer HTML workspace, and a build manifest with source and output hashes.
operational_fields.success_criteria: All requested files are generated, deterministic checks pass, the HTML is self-contained, and comments remain bound to stable node IDs.
operational_fields.failure_criteria: A hash mismatch, incomplete locale, invalid review state, projection drift, unsafe external dependency, or broken HTML interaction is found.
operational_fields.failure_action: Route the exact projection defect to B02-F01; return to B01 only if the canonical graph or concept is responsible.
operational_fields.executor: Local CLI and browser-native review workspace, orchestrated by a compatible coding agent when requested.
operational_fields.acceptor: User or product owner reviewing the generated workspace and recorded build evidence.
operational_fields.resources: Python standard library, modern browser, canonical graph, optional locale and presentation files.
operational_fields.evidence: Generated artifacts, OPERATION-MAP-BUILD.json, test results, and optional browser smoke evidence.
operational_fields.next_transition: A passing projection gate closes this module workflow and returns reviewed evidence to the broader product process.

nodes.title: Local review workspace and build manifest

operational_fields.what: Verify generated files, source hashes, self-contained HTML behavior, and the separation between canonical graph and review annotations.
operational_fields.where: The package test suite, build manifest, and optional desktop and mobile browser smoke run.
operational_fields.when: After projection generation and before the result is presented as a usable review workspace.
operational_fields.why: File existence alone does not prove that the graph is visible, navigable, local, portable, or bound to the correct source.
operational_fields.input: Generated projections, build manifest, canonical graph, review-state contract, and test environment.
operational_fields.input_requirements: Artifact hashes resolve, deterministic tests have run, and skipped browser checks remain explicitly marked as not run.
operational_fields.expected_output: An accepted self-contained workspace or a bounded projection failure report.
operational_fields.success_criteria: Tests pass, manifest hashes match, no remote asset is required, and review data remains separate from canonical graph meaning.
operational_fields.failure_criteria: Any output is stale, missing, externally dependent, unsafe, visually unusable, or bound to another graph revision.
operational_fields.failure_action: Open B02-F01 with the exact failed artifact or check and rebuild only after its source is corrected.
operational_fields.executor: Package test runner and, when available, browser smoke runner.
operational_fields.acceptor: User or product owner receiving the local review workspace.
operational_fields.resources: Generated files, package tests, build manifest, and modern browser when visual verification is available.
operational_fields.evidence: Passing test output, matching hashes, accepted build manifest, and explicit browser-check state.
operational_fields.next_transition: Success reaches END; failure opens B02-F01.

operational_fields.what: Record a projection, localization, review-state, security, or visual failure without mutating canonical product meaning.
operational_fields.where: The failed build output, test evidence, or browser-smoke report for B02.
operational_fields.when: Whenever projection acceptance rejects generated artifacts or their observable behavior.
operational_fields.why: Projection defects must be repaired at the presentation boundary unless evidence proves the canonical graph is responsible.
operational_fields.input: Exact failed artifact, check result, source hashes, and reproduction command.
operational_fields.input_requirements: The failure is reproducible and classified as projection-owned or explicitly routed upstream with evidence.
operational_fields.expected_output: A bounded renderer, catalog, manifest, or review-state correction followed by regenerated projections.
operational_fields.success_criteria: The responsible output is corrected, all affected projections are regenerated, and acceptance checks pass.
operational_fields.failure_criteria: The correction edits generated output manually, changes canonical meaning, hides a skipped check, or introduces a remote dependency.
operational_fields.failure_action: Return to B02 with the reproducible failure; escalate to B01 only for a proven canonical-source defect.
operational_fields.executor: Responsible projection maintainer or compatible coding agent.
operational_fields.acceptor: User or product owner after regenerated evidence passes.
operational_fields.resources: Build manifest, renderer source, locale catalog, tests, and browser evidence when available.
operational_fields.evidence: Failure reproduction, corrected source diff, regenerated artifact hashes, and passing acceptance output.
operational_fields.next_transition: Return to B02 and regenerate all affected projections from canonical inputs.
